idea.hook: One giant planet is spinning... SIDEWAYS?!
facts: Most planets in our solar system spin relatively upright to their orbit, like Earth (23.5° axial tilt).
But Uranus spins on its side, with an astounding axial tilt of 97.8 degrees!
This means its poles face the Sun for 42 Earth years at a time, causing extreme, decades-long seasons.
idea.payoff: Scientists believe a massive, ancient collision early in its history literally knocked Uranus over!
idea.title: Uranus's Sideways Spin! 🤯🪐

scenes.text: ONE GIANT PLANET IS SPINNING... SIDEWAYS?!
scenes.visual: Deep space background. A stylized planet icon slowly rotates, then dramatically tilts nearly 90 degrees.

scenes.text: Most planets spin upright, like Earth (23.5° tilt).
scenes.visual: Deep space background. Earth icon spinning with a slight 23.5° tilt, labeled 'Earth'.

scenes.text: But Uranus spins on its side! An astounding 97.8° axial tilt!
scenes.visual: Deep space background. Uranus icon slides in, dramatically spinning on its side (nearly 90 degrees). Labeled 'Uranus'.

scenes.text: Its poles face the Sun for 42 Earth years. Decades-long seasons!
scenes.visual: Deep space background. Uranus still on its side. A glowing sun appears, casting direct light onto one of Uranus's poles. A subtle '42 Years' timer visual appears.

scenes.text: A massive, ancient collision literally knocked Uranus over!
scenes.visual: Deep space background. Two planetoids collide violently, one (Uranus) is visibly knocked onto its side after the impact, debris flies briefly.
